sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated Sequencing.com marketplace detail-page parity is still pending.
No calibrated sleep-duration, chronotype, sleep-quality, or sleep-disorder probability model is supplied with this fixture.
No sleep diary, wearable data, medical history, medication list, sleep study, ferritin or iron lab result, caffeine history, or symptom history is supplied.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived CLOCK/PER1/CRY1 circadian marker summary is available. This is biological-clock context, not a chronotype diagnosis.
resultRows.sourceLabel: NIGMS circadian rhythms / MedlinePlus healthy sleep
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Circadian rhythms help shape sleep timing, but light exposure, schedule, stress, activity, food timing, social environment, and health context also matter.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A summary-level ABCC9 sleep-duration context row is available. This does not predict how many hours of sleep are needed.
resultRows.sourceLabel: MedlinePlus healthy sleep / NHGRI polygenic risk scores
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Sleep duration is a complex trait. This row supports educational context only, not a personalized sleep-hours target.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A summary-level P2RY2/PAX8/VRK2 sleep-quality context row is available. Symptoms and sleep disorders are not inferred.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Sleep problems can have many causes, including health conditions, medications, genetics, schedules, caffeine, alcohol, aging, and environment.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample overview shows Sleep Duration as Typical, Sleep Quality as Typical, and Deep Sleep as Typical.
sampleRows.description: The visible card descriptions say likely typical sleep duration, likely typical sleep quality, and likely a typical sleeper.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the rendered public Sleep DNA sample PDF overview page; this is observed sample output, not local-user sleep scoring.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Sample result: typical likelihood of insomnia.
sampleRows.description: The overview page displays the Insomnia card under Sleep Disturbances with a Typical Likelihood label.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample risk-score card only; do not convert this into a local diagnosis, symptom inference, sleep-study decision, or treatment guidance.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Sample result: more likely to move during sleep.
sampleRows.description: The overview page displays the Sleep Movement card under Sleep Disturbances with a More Likely label.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample risk-score card only; do not infer local symptoms or recommend iron, supplements, devices, or clinical actions from genotype alone.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Sample result: more likely to have restless legs syndrome.
sampleRows.description: The overview page displays the Restless Legs card under Sleep Disturbances with a More Likely label.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample card wording only; the local report must not diagnose restless legs syndrome or prescribe iron therapy.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Sample result: typical likelihood of sleep apnea.
sampleRows.description: The overview page displays the Sleep Apnea card under Sleep Disturbances with a Typical Likelihood label.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample risk-score card only; do not use genotype to replace sleep apnea screening, testing, or clinician evaluation.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Sample result: more likely to snore.
sampleRows.description: The overview page displays the Snoring card under Sleep Disturbances with a More Likely label.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample risk-score card only; do not infer local snoring symptoms, sleep apnea status, or treatment need.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Sample result: less likely to experience daytime sleepiness.
sampleRows.description: The overview page displays the Daytime Sleepiness card under Sleep Disturbances with a Less Likely label.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample risk-score card only; do not infer local alertness, fatigue, occupational safety, or clinical status.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample overview shows Early Bird or Night Owl as Early Bird and Napping as Less Likely.
sampleRows.description: The visible card descriptions say more likely to be an early bird and less likely to take naps.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample behavior-card structure only; do not quantify local chronotype, napping probability, work schedule advice, or medical conclusions.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample recommendation says Iron helps with Restless Legs and Sleep Movement, shows impact/evidence ratings, and includes personalized-to-your-genes variant panels.
sampleRows.description: Visible sample variant tables include GLO1 rs3923809 AA, MEIS1 rs2300478 GG, and an unlabeled rs855791 AA row while recommendation text refers to BTBD9, MEIS1, and TMPRSS6-linked iron context.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed recommendation and sample variant-table structure only; do not convert it into local iron supplementation, ferritin testing, restless-legs diagnosis, or treatment advice.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample recommendation says Reduce Caffeine Intake helps with Sleep Movement and Insomnia, with additional Sleep Quality and Sleep Duration pages using the same MTNR1B sample variant row.
sampleRows.description: Visible sample variant panels show MTNR1B rs10830964 CC and recommendation text about caffeine, adenosine, melatonin, and timing before bedtime.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed recommendation and sample variant-table structure only; do not convert it into local caffeine restriction, insomnia treatment, safety advice, or medical guidance.

plainEnglishLimits: This report provides educational sleep wellness context only.
Public sample rows show the shape of the Sleep DNA report and are not local-user sleep findings.
Genetic context does not diagnose insomnia, narcolepsy, sleep apnea, restless legs, bruxism, bedwetting, depression, anxiety, or another sleep or mental-health condition.
Sleep is influenced by genetics, health, age, schedule, light exposure, environment, stress, activity, medications, caffeine, alcohol, and habits.
sleepStudyAndCareBoundaries: This report does not replace a sleep study or clinician evaluation.
This report does not recommend CPAP, medications, supplements, melatonin, devices, caffeine changes, iron therapy, ferritin testing, or treatment changes.
Persistent sleep problems, severe daytime sleepiness, breathing problems during sleep, or safety concerns require medical evaluation.

references.title: NHGRI overview of polygenic risk scores

genotypeSummary.genotype: derived circadian rhythm marker context available
genotypeSummary.effect: The local fixture supplies summary-level circadian context without a calibrated chronotype score.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Report as sleep wellness context only; do not diagnose a sleep disorder, infer symptoms, or recommend treatment.

genotypeSummary.effect: The local fixture supplies summary-level sleep-duration context but no sleep-hours prediction.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Report as context only; keep probabilities, calibration status, and uncertainty in the appendix.

genotypeSummary.genotype: GLO1 rs3923809 AA; MEIS1 rs2300478 GG; unlabeled rs855791 AA
genotypeSummary.effect: The public Sleep DNA sample displays these rows in Iron recommendation variant tables tied to restless legs and sleep movement content.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample-output structure only; do not infer local iron status, supplement need, restless legs syndrome, or treatment action.

genotypeSummary.effect: The public Sleep DNA sample displays MTNR1B rs10830964 CC in Reduce Caffeine Intake recommendation pages.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample-output structure only; do not infer local caffeine sensitivity, insomnia, sleep duration, sleep quality, or medical advice.

genotypeSummary.genotype: Typical sleep duration; typical sleep quality; typical deep sleep; typical insomnia; more likely sleep movement; more likely restless legs; typical sleep apnea; more likely snoring; less likely daytime sleepiness; typical caffeine-related sleep problems; early bird; less likely napping
genotypeSummary.effect: The public sample demonstrates risk-score card categories, labels, and short result sentences across sleep parameters, disturbances, and behaviors.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as sample report structure only; do not emit calibrated local risk scores unless a validated model with calibration and provenance is supplied.

missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace detail-page parity has not been extracted.
No calibrated sleep-duration, chronotype, sleep-quality, sleepiness, sleep apnea, insomnia, or restless-legs model is supplied.
No sleep diary, wearable data, medication list, clinical sleep history, caffeine intake history, iron or ferritin labs, or sleep-study result is supplied.
uncertainty: The fixture uses summary-level marker evidence for validation and is not a complete sleep-genetics model.
Public sample cards and recommendation blocks are sample-output examples, not local-user probabilities or instructions.
The effect of individual markers may vary across populations and should not be used as a diagnosis or treatment guide.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and does not diagnose sleep disorders, infer symptoms, prescribe sleep aids, recommend caffeine changes, recommend iron therapy, or replace a sleep study.
Sequencing.com marketplace detail-page parity is still pending even though the public sample PDF rows are extracted.
